pattern: Only the current set of occupied positions ever matters, never which specific marble sits where, so replaying the moves against a set -- vacate the source, occupy the destination -- tracks the final layout directly.
hints: You never need to know which individual marble is where -- only which positions currently have at least one marble on them.
Keep a set of currently-occupied positions; for each move, the source position becomes empty (every marble there moves away together), so remove it from the set, then add the destination position.

Because a position vacated by one move can become occupied again later through a different move, apply the moves strictly in the given order against the same running set, then read off whatever remains at the end.

edge_inputs.description: moves converge onto a single final position
a position is vacated then re-occupied by a later move
every marble moves to the exact same destination
positions at the minimum and maximum allowed values
a move targets a position that a marble already occupies
a chain of moves cycles back through an earlier position

stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    from collections import Counter
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    count = Counter(nums)
    moveFrom = []
    moveTo = []
    for _ in range(n):
        positions = list(count.keys())
        f = rng.choice(positions)
        t = rng.randint(1, 1000000000)
        moveFrom.append(f)
        moveTo.append(t)
        c = count.pop(f)
        count[t] = count.get(t, 0) + c
    return [nums, moveFrom, moveTo]
